name: Ojos Negros - Sierra Menera Trail
name_original: Ojos Negros - Sierra Manera – circular por las Minas de Hierro (PR-TE 52)
description: Hike the Ojos Negros - Sierra Menera Trail, a 9.2-mile moderate route exploring Spain's mining heritage and diverse landscapes.
region_intro: Hiking around Ojos Negros offers diverse landscapes, from the plains of Teruel to the mountainous Sierra de Javalambre. The region is characterized by green valleys, gentle hills, and meandering rivers, providing varied terrain for outdoor activities. Notable features include the Vía Verde de Ojos Negros, a former mining railway line with historical bridges and tunnels, and areas with pine woods and kermes oak forests. This varied environment provides a range of hiking experiences. Best hiking trails around Ojos Negros The most popular hiking route is Peracense Castle Loop, a 2.5 miles (4.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 2 minutes to complete, offering views of the historic castle. Another top favourite among local hikers is Ojos Negros - Sierra Menera Trail, a moderate 9.2 miles (14.8 km) path. This route traverses varied terrain, including parts of the Sierra Menera mountain range. Local hikers also love the Pozuel del Campo and Ojos Negros Loop, an 8.5 miles (13.7 km) trail leading through rural landscapes and villages, often completed in about 3 hours 47 minutes. Hiking around Ojos Negros is defined by green valleys, historical railway infrastructure, and varied mountain scenery. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to moderate trails. The routes in Ojos Negros are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.75 stars from over 3 reviews. More than 17 hikers have used komoot to explore Ojos Negros's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Ojos Negros?
A: There are several hiking trails around Ojos Negros. Komoot lists 7 routes, ranging from easy to moderate difficulty, offering diverse experiences across the region's landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Ojos Negros?
A: The terrain around Ojos Negros is quite varied. You can expect to hike through green valleys, gentle hills, and expansive open plains, particularly in the Teruel area. The landscape also features pine woods and kermes oak forests, with views of mountain ranges like Sierra Menera and Sierra de Javalambre. Many routes follow the historic Vía Verde de Ojos Negros, characterized by former railway lines, bridges, and tunnels.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Ojos Negros?
A: Yes, there are easy hiking trails suitable for beginners. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979581393/peracense-castle-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peracense Castle Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 2.5-mile (4.0 km) trail that takes about an hour to complete, offering scenic views of the historic castle.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Ojos Negros?
A: Many of the trails along the Vía Verde de Ojos Negros are suitable for families due to their relatively flat and car-free nature. The diverse scenery and historical elements like bridges and tunnels can keep children engaged. Shorter, easy loops such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979581393/peracense-castle-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peracense Castle Loop&lt;/a&gt; are also good choices for families.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Ojos Negros?
A: Ojos Negros benefits from a moderate continental climate, making it suitable for hiking throughout most of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for exploring the trails, with comfortable conditions and vibrant natural scenery. Summers can be warm, while winters are cooler but often still suitable for hiking.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Ojos Negros?
A: Yes, there are circular hiking routes available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979572694/pozuel-del-campo-and-ojos-negros-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozuel del Campo and Ojos Negros Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 8.5-mile (13.7 km) trail that takes you through rural landscapes and villages, returning to your starting point. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31478828" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojos Negros Mill loop from Ojos Negros&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 5.1-mile (8.2 km) route.

Q: What interesting landmarks or attractions can I see along the hiking trails?
A: Hiking in Ojos Negros offers access to several interesting landmarks. You can explore the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3925682" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peracense Castle&lt;/a&gt;, a medieval fortress. The Vía Verde itself features impressive historical infrastructure, including spectacular stone bridges, viaducts, and tunnels. You can also visit the restored 16th-17th century &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4852508" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojos Negros Mill&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any villages or towns to explore near the hiking routes?
A: Yes, the hiking routes often pass through or near charming villages. While hiking, you might encounter places like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5098803" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozuel del Campo&lt;/a&gt; or the town of Ojos Negros itself, which offers cultural and natural attractions. The broader region includes picturesque villages such as Caudiel, Jérica, and Navajas, known for their historical charm.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Ojos Negros?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.75 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, the historical elements of the Vía Verde, and the varied terrain that offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to moderate trails.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging hikes in the Ojos Negros region?
A: While there are no 'difficult' graded routes listed, several moderate trails offer longer distances and more elevation gain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e981452479/ojos-negros-sierra-menera-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojos Negros - Sierra Menera Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 9.2-mile (14.8 km) path that traverses varied terrain, including parts of the Sierra Menera mountain range, providing a more extended hiking experience.

Q: Can I find waterfalls or significant water features on hikes in Ojos Negros?
A: While the immediate Ojos Negros area features meandering rivers like the Palancia, the broader region is known for the stunning 'El Salto de la Novia' (Bride's Leap) waterfall near Navajas. This 60-meter waterfall is a notable natural feature in the wider vicinity, though not directly on the listed Ojos Negros trails.

Q: Is there public transport available to access hiking trails in Ojos Negros?
A: Information on specific public transport links directly to trailheads in Ojos Negros is limited. However, the Vía Verde de Ojos Negros, being a former railway line, often has access points near towns that may be served by regional bus routes. It is advisable to check local transport schedules for connections to villages like Ojos Negros or nearby towns.

Q: Where can I park when hiking in Ojos Negros?
A: Parking availability typically varies by trailhead. For routes starting in or near villages like Ojos Negros or Peracense, you can usually find parking in designated village car parks or along streets, respecting local regulations. For trails along the Vía Verde, look for access points near towns, which often have small parking areas.
tips: An icon of the town of Ojos Negros, this mill dates back to the 16th-17th century and today can be seen fully restored. You can enter it and inside see its different rooms that will allow you to understand how this wonderful work of engineering worked. The warehouse is located on the ground floor and all the machinery was located above it.

These mines, now abandoned, have been exploited since Roman and Muslim times, although massively only in the 20th century with the technological advances of the time. During the Civil War the works were abandoned and then resumed around 1941 until their final abandonment in 1987 even though there were abundant reserves of this mineral.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level and typical duration for hiking the Ojos Negros - Sierra Menera Trail?
faqs.answer: This specific route, PR-TE 52 Ojos Negros - Sierra Menera, is considered moderate. It covers approximately 14.8 km with an elevation gain of about 238m and a similar loss. You can expect to complete it in around 4 hours.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain will I encounter on this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail primarily follows an old narrow-gauge railway line, so the terrain is generally characterized by compacted soil with gentle gradients. While mostly flat, there are occasional slopes. You'll also traverse numerous tunnels and viaducts, which are notable features of the route.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the Ojos Negros - Sierra Menera Trail?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll discover a blend of natural beauty and industrial heritage. Key highlights include the Ojos Negros Mill and the historical Ojos Negros iron mines. The trail also features impressive viaducts, tunnels, and the dramatic, multi-colored landscape around the former open-pit mines.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to hike the Ojos Negros - Sierra Menera Trail?
faqs.answer: The most ideal times to experience the beautiful landscapes and comfortable climate are between March and June, and again from September to November. These periods offer pleasant weather for hiking.

faqs.question: Is the Ojos Negros - Sierra Menera Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through towns, near livestock, or in areas with other trail users.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a permit or have to pay an entrance fee to hike the Ojos Negros - Sierra Menera Trail. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Ojos Negros - Sierra Menera Trail?
faqs.answer: Specific parking information for the start of the PR-TE 52 route around Ojos Negros is best found by checking local maps or community resources for designated parking areas near the village of Ojos Negros, which serves as a common access point for this section of the greenway.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect to see along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers a diverse and surprising landscape. You'll pass through plains, mountainous regions with Mediterranean pine forests, oak, and sabine trees. The route also winds through charming towns and showcases significant industrial archaeology from the mining era, including the old railway infrastructure itself.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is part of the larger Vía Verde Ojos Negros, which is Spain's longest greenway. While hiking this specific section, you will also be on parts of the official trails named "Ojos Negros - Sierra Menera" and "Sierra Menera".

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restaurants nearby?
faqs.answer: The trail passes through or near charming towns and villages, offering opportunities to appreciate local gastronomy. While specific establishments aren't listed, you can often find cafes and restaurants in the villages along the route, such as Ojos Negros itself, to refuel.

faqs.question: Is the Ojos Negros - Sierra Menera Trail a circular route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the specific route highlighted, "Ojos Negros - Sierra Menera Trail", is designed as a circular route, allowing you to start and finish in the same general area.
